full_prompt: Design a wing-mounted payload pylon for a Group 3 tactical UAS (MTOW 320 kg, cruise Mach 0.25 at 10,000 ft), sized to Air Force university-design-challenge-style requirements inherited from MIL-HDBK-516C Part V airframe structural criteria and MIL-STD-810H mechanical environmental tests. The pylon carries a 30 kg external payload (sensor pod) suspended 0.4 m below the wing lower skin, with mounting to the wing via three 3/8-inch Hi-Lok fasteners at a 120 mm x 80 mm pattern into a reinforced wing-rib lug. Pylon material: Aluminum 7075-T6 (Young's modulus 71.7 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 503 MPa, ultimate 572 MPa, density 2810 kg/m^3). Pylon envelope 300 mm length x 120 mm chord x 0.4 m depth. Load cases per MIL-HDBK-516C: (LC1) 1 g static + 7 g pull-up maneuver limit = 8 g = 240 N per kg = 7.2 kN per 30 kg payload vertical at pin; (LC2) ultimate: 1.5 x LC1 = 10.8 kN; (LC3) 4 g side maneuver lateral = 1.2 kN at CG 0.4 m below wing; (LC4) air-to-ground bomb separation kick: 500 N vertical release impulse for 100 ms; (LC5) vibration qualification per MIL-STD-810H Method 514.8 Category 4 (Propeller Aircraft) - overall 2.0 Grms, 5-2000 Hz, 1 hr per axis; (LC6) temperature: -40 C to +85 C operational, -55 C to +71 C storage; (LC7) fatigue: 10^5 cycles of 0-7.2 kN pin load representing 500 mission-hour service life. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises <= 335 MPa (503 / 1.5 yield) under LC1-LC4; (R2) ultimate LC2 <= 382 MPa (572 / 1.5 ultimate); (R3) random-vib 3-sigma stress under LC5 <= 335 MPa; (R4) fatigue alternating stress at fastener hole <= 150 MPa (7075-T6 notched Kt=3 endurance at 10^5); (R5) pylon mass <= 2.5 kg; (R6) first pylon + payload pitching mode above 20 Hz to avoid flutter coupling. Deliverables: STEP, FEA per case, random-vib and fatigue summaries, modal, and mass report.
short_prompt:
Design a wing-mounted payload pylon for a Group 3 tactical UAS (MTOW 320 kg, cruise Mach 0.25 at 10,000 ft), carrying a 30 kg sensor pod suspended 0.4 m below the wing lower skin via three 3/8-inch Hi-Lok fasteners into a reinforced wing-rib lug on a 120 x 80 mm pattern. Material class: 7075-T6 aluminum. Qualify per MIL-HDBK-516C Part V airframe criteria and MIL-STD-810H mechanical environmental tests (Method 514.8 Category 4 propeller aircraft vibration).

Envelope: pylon on the order of 300 mm length x 120 mm chord x 0.4 m depth; pylon mass <= 2.5 kg.

Load cases.
- LC1 8 g limit pull-up (1 g + 7 g): 7.2 kN vertical at the pin.
- LC2 ultimate 1.5 x LC1: 10.8 kN.
- LC3 4 g side: 1.2 kN lateral at the CG 0.4 m below the wing.
- LC4 separation kick: 500 N vertical for 100 ms on release.
- LC5 random vibration: MIL-STD-810H M514.8 Cat 4 at 2.0 Grms, 5-2000 Hz, 1 hr per axis.
- LC6 thermal: -40 to +85 C operational, -55 to +71 C storage.
- LC7 fatigue: 1e5 cycles of 0-7.2 kN pin load (500 mission-hour life).

prompt.title: Group-3 tactical UAS payload pylon
prompt.brief: 7075-T6 Al pylon for 30 kg sensor pod under 8 g limit + MIL-STD-810H vibration qual.

notes.needs_rulebook_fetch_reason: Air Force university-design-challenge problem statement is not public; numerics grounded in MIL-HDBK-516C + MIL-STD-810H defaults.
